_meta.description: H100 Stage-1 launch convergence record (attempts + blocker resolution chain + live pod)

convergence.final_status: LIVE+BOOTSTRAPPED+DRILL_PASS + P13 RESOLVED + P12 RESOLVED (AN11(a) live PASS) — pod 85mbtwbruechza H100 SXM 11:14Z-11:25Z, ALM r13 PEFT LoRA gpt2 100-step (3.74s wall), adapter rank=8, delta_norm=1.01311. Verifier nth_positional refactored to real_args() (P13 follow-on). Stage-2 prereqs satisfied (AN11(a) PASS + cell-eigenvec-16 VERIFIED).

attempts.name: anima-stage1-alm-r13
attempts.outcome: FAIL — runpodctl --gpu-type flag deprecated (runpodctl 1.x uses --gpuType). No kickoff command delivered to pod.

attempts.name: anima-stage1-alm-r13
attempts.outcome: FAIL — SSH succeeded · pod running H100 80GB HBM3 · hexa-lang cloned inside pod · BUT self/native/hexa_v2 is ARM64 (Mac) binary · 'Exec format error' on Linux x86_64. No Linux pre-built hexa binary in hexa-lang repo.

attempts.name: anima-stage1-alm-r13
attempts.outcome: LIVE — Stage-1 pod bootstrapped successfully. All 5 convergence issues resolved prior to retry.
attempts.env_highlights: ANIMA_STAGE=1 · ROADMAP_ENTRY=9 · HEXA_STRICT=1 · ALM_CONFIG=config/alm_r13_v2_config.json · TORCH_COMPILE=1 · bf16 · GRAD_ACCUM=4 · CKPT_INTERVAL=25 · IDLE_KILL_MIN=5

issues.root_cause: Manifest stored kickoff commands used runpodctl 0.x camelCase flags (--gpuType / --imageName / --secureCloud / --startSSH / --cost / --env K=V). runpodctl 1.x rejects with 'unknown flag'.
issues.impact: All 5 stored kickoff commands (stage1 + stage2_p1..p4) non-executable.
issues.resolution: anima 62889155 — migrate all 5 to kebab-case (--gpu-id / --image / --cloud-type SECURE / --container-disk-in-gb / --volume-in-gb / --volume-mount-path / --env JSON object). --cost removed (no 1.x equivalent; guard lives in abort_thresholds). --startSSH removed (SSH default on).

issues.root_cause: hexa-lang repo self/native/hexa_v2 baseline binary is Mac ARM64. No install.sh / Linux bootstrap. Linux parity (O75) flagged ENV_BLOCKED.
issues.impact: All H100 pod kickoffs impossible until Linux hexa binary is available (4-repo cross_repo_blocker).
issues.resolution: hexa-lang 1fdc0100 — Linux x86_64 static musl 5.5MB ELF committed to build/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64.

issues.root_cause: Legacy hexa_v2 transpiler leaks bare `now()` / `push(arr,v)` / `setenv` / `exec_capture` identifiers into generated .c as hexa_call{0,1,2} operands — runtime has only hx_*-prefixed globals.
issues.impact: AOT-compiled anima tools fail clang link. anima required sed-fixup workaround (tool/build_fixup_native.bash) for every native build.
issues.resolution: hexa-lang 373696e7 — runtime.c terminal #define shim extended (hxa-004 ext): `now → timestamp`, `push → hx_push` (new TAG_FN + _w_push wrapper around hexa_array_push). anima 2f6c8e60 — doc §1 RESOLVED + workaround marked OBSOLETE.

issues.root_cause: tool/anima_cli/compute.hexa parsed `runpodctl pod list` as table (tail -n +2 | wc -l) when 1.x emits JSON array. Counted 10 JSON lines for 1 pod.
issues.impact: `anima compute status` reported 'pods: 10 running' when only 1 pod was live — misleading operator display and incorrect `stop all` target extraction.
issues.resolution: anima 69b5bdb6 — switch to `jq 'length'` for count and `jq -r '.[].id'` for stop-all id extraction.

issues.root_cause: state/h100_stage1_launch_state.json final_status = 'ABORTED — pod bootstrap blocker (hexa baseline binary architecture mismatch)' lingered after successful attempt 3 launch.
issues.impact: `anima compute status` / downstream dashboards displayed a blocker message that no longer applied.
issues.resolution: anima a200b387 — attempt_3 appended, final_status → LIVE, blocker_diagnosis marked RESOLVED. This file now restructured under `convergence` attr (schema v2).

issues.root_cause: pod_bootstrap_sequence referenced `https://<R2>/hexa-linux-x86_64` placeholder. Binary existed locally but not in R2.
issues.impact: Pod could not fetch hexa binary to self-bootstrap.
issues.resolution: anima 55bdad56 — rclone copy to r2:anima-models/bin/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 + tool/fetch_hexa_binary_url.bash (7-day presigned URL on demand, anima-models is private) + bootstrap_sequence rewritten with 3-step flow.

issues.root_cause: runpod/pytorch:2.4.0-py3.11-cuda12.4.1-devel-ubuntu22.04 ships gcc but not clang. hexa runtime macro block uses some clang-specific flags and stdlib include paths under -Wno-trigraphs + -fbracket-depth=4096.
issues.impact: hexa wrapper's compile step failed with 'clang: command not found' on first invocation.
issues.resolution: In-pod `apt-get update && apt-get install -y clang` (one-shot, captured into pod-side cache). Future pods either install on demand or build a custom image with clang pre-installed (landmine captured).

issues.root_cause: hexa-lang build/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 is the *transpiler* only (hexa-cc). Mac ships a Rust driver binary at /opt/homebrew/bin/hexa that does transpile → clang → exec for `hexa run <file>`; no Linux equivalent exists upstream.
issues.impact: First pod invocation `hexa run tool/drill_breakthrough_runner.hexa ...` was parsed by hexa-cc as `input=run, output=tool/drill_breakthrough_runner.hexa`, *overwriting the source file* with transpiled C.
issues.resolution: (a) git restore tool/drill_breakthrough_runner.hexa. (b) Rename the transpiler to /usr/local/bin/hexa_v2. (c) Install /usr/local/bin/hexa — a bash wrapper that accepts `hexa run <file>` and `hexa <file>` (Mac CLI compat) and does transpile → clang -I /workspace/hexa-lang/self → exec, with content-hash cache at /tmp/hexa_cache. Recipe captured in pod_bootstrap_sequence for re-use on future pods. Upstream: hexa-lang roadmap 67 (M5 — self-hosted Linux driver `hexa_driver.hexa`), tracked under parent 64 / hxa-20260423-003.

issues.root_cause: anima tooling resolves canonical paths like drill_breakthrough_criteria via config/drill_ext_resolver.json, which expands ~ → $HOME and expects ~/core/nexus/bench/... ~/core/hexa-lang/self/... etc. Fresh pod had /root/core empty.
issues.impact: `hexa run tool/drill_ext_resolve.hexa drill_breakthrough_criteria` exited non-zero, which made drill_breakthrough_runner report 'not resolved'.
issues.resolution: mkdir -p /root/core; git clone nexus + airgenome; symlink /workspace/anima + /workspace/hexa-lang into /root/core/. All 4 sister repos now resolvable from $HOME/core/. Captured as step in pod_bootstrap_sequence.

issues.root_cause: drill_breakthrough_runner's `ext_resolve` uses exec("hexa tool/drill_ext_resolve.hexa <ref>") — implicit `run` (Mac CLI convention). Initial wrapper only accepted explicit `hexa run <file>`, so the exec'd shell saw the wrapper's usage-message on stderr and reported 'not resolved'.
issues.impact: drill_breakthrough_runner refused to start even with prereqs satisfied.
issues.resolution: Wrapper amended: `if first arg ends in .hexa and exists, rewrite argv to `run <file> …`. Verified with `cd /root/core/anima && hexa tool/drill_ext_resolve.hexa drill_breakthrough_criteria` → prints resolved absolute path.

issues.root_cause: drill_breakthrough_runner defaults to strict_audit=true; refuses to run without state/drill_criteria_audit.jsonl. Fresh pod has no audit log (genesis).
issues.impact: First drill attempt exited with 'audit: missing … — refusing to run (strict)'.
issues.resolution: Use `--all-seeds` flag which sets strict_audit=false (genesis mode). Captured in pod_bootstrap_sequence. Eventually Stage-2 workflow should seed the audit log from an approved baseline run.

issues.root_cause: self/native/hexa_v2 binary (Mac + Linux) is pre-4a5e57a5. Any codegen fixes landing upstream (e.g. .last_index_of dispatch which is actually already wired at source) do not reach anima until the binary is rebuilt. hexa cc --regen merges 4 SSOT modules and hits __hexa_strlit_init per-module symbol collision (Phase C.2 namespacing unimplemented). Not a one-turn fix.
issues.impact: anima workarounds (tool/an11_a_verifier.hexa::_last_idx_char, nth_positional dup-detect, tool/hexa_linux_shim.bash) must remain in place. P12 bridge scaffold cannot produce a valid rank>=1 output locally until the binary reflects current codegen.
issues.upstream_tracker: hexa-lang roadmap 66 (M4 codegen completion) exit_criterion (1). Parent roadmap 64, siblings 65 (M3 argv) / 67 (M5 Linux driver) / 68 (M2 mangling) / 69 (M1 runtime split).
issues.anima_workaround_removal_trigger: On hexa-lang binary rebuild landing (self/native/hexa_v2 + build/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 both updated past 4a5e57a5 AND Phase C.2 resolved): delete _last_idx_char helper + nth_positional dup-detect branch in tool/an11_a_verifier.hexa; remove install-time hexa_linux_shim recipe from pod_bootstrap_sequence; re-upload fresh binary to r2:anima-models/bin/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 via rclone + bump fetch_hexa_binary_url.bash's EXPECTED_SHA.
issues.resolution: hexa-lang upstream roadmap 66 Phase C.2 LANDED: 968900b8 fixed __hexa_sl_* + __hexa_strlit_init per-module namespacing (v3==v4 byte-identical self-hosting closure); 4238b758 + 388eece8 rebuilt build/hexa_v2_linux (1.69 MiB, sha 42d01578...) including M4 pad_start/pad_end codegen. anima side: rclone copy to r2:anima-models/bin/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 (same path, new content), tool/fetch_hexa_binary_url.bash EXPECTED_SHA bumped to 42d01578... and source header updated with deprecation trail. Next pod bootstrap now pulls the Phase-C.2 binary.
issues.followon_workaround_removal: Source-level removal of tool/an11_a_verifier.hexa::_last_idx_char + nth_positional dup-detect branch is NOT done yet — requires a live pod test with the new binary to verify .last_index_of() + real_args() dispatch before deleting workarounds. Grep-able removal stubs already inlined at anima 9961be1f.

followon_progress.detail: _last_idx_char helper (20 LOC) deleted + 2 call sites migrated to native .last_index_of() after verifying dispatch on Mac stage0 self/native/hexa_v2 rebuilt today. Selftest runs clean (synthetic rank=0 FAIL is unchanged pre-existing behavior, not a regression). Still pending on pod: nth_positional dup-detect branch removal (needs live-pod real_args() verification).

stage1_drill_breakthrough.fixpoint_3_of_4_is_by_design: seed_neg_01 has kind=negative / expected=absorption — the falsification substrate REQUIRES this seed to NOT reach fixpoint. A 4/4 fixpoint result would indicate the negative seed was erroneously absorbed into a fixpoint, falsifying the test. 3/4 fixpoint with the absorbed seed being seed_neg_01 is the designed PASS shape. Criteria fail_case = 'absorption_rate >= 0.3 OR saturation_state != fixpoint OR ANY module < depth_min OR cross_run_mismatch' — positive seeds must fixpoint, the negative seed must not. verdict=PASS correctly reflects the pass=4/4 (each seed matched expected behavior).
stage1_drill_breakthrough.source: pod mqljodjb5pqpk4 (/root/core/anima) — first live drill output on Linux x86_64 H100

pending_items.root_cause: Stage-2 ready_conditions[0] wants state/alm_r13_an11_a_live.json — the AN11(a) 'weight_emergent' live empirical verdict. Emitter chain: (1) LoRA training produces new ckpt; (2) an11_a_verifier diffs base vs round; (3) operator promotes to _live.json with pod provenance.
pending_items.progress_this_session: tool/alm_r13_train.hexa (DRAFT, anima 2026-04-23) lands the orchestration contract: arg schema, env-selected backend (ANIMA_TRAIN_BACKEND ∈ {pytorch, mlx, stub}), exit code map, post-train verify step, explicit non-auto-promotion of _live. Selftest PASS (config load + schema check). Stub backend dry-run PASS. Pytorch + MLX backend drivers (tool/alm_r13_backend_{pytorch,mlx}.py) NOT yet authored — that is the remaining work to unblock Stage-2.
pending_items.impact: Stage-2 cascade still correctly blocked. But: the landing pad for a concrete backend now exists; any future PyTorch/MLX driver just needs to match the CLI the orchestrator already invokes.
pending_items.next_step: Pod-side install (HEXA-FIRST: no .py in repo). docs/pod_bootstrap_checklist_20260423.md §2.5 now contains the tool/alm_r13_backend_mlx.py heredoc — installs during pod bootstrap. Next: when ready to verify end-to-end, run the install+dry-run verification commands from §2.5 in a live pod, then lift the dry-run flag for a real train → verify → promote cycle. For pytorch variant (real PEFT+quant): add a parallel heredoc in §2.5 — same pod-install pattern, no repo commit.
non_fixes_considered_and_rejected: Running an11_a_verifier --dry-run: emits synthetic FAIL by design — not a PASS.
Running anima_learning_free_driver on pod: Path β explicitly expects AN11(a) FAIL as the no-train signature.
Manually promoting drill_breakthrough_results.json → alm_r13_an11_a_live.json: different metric (substrate vs weight_emergent); would corrupt the cascade gate's integrity.
Emitting via stub backend: stub writes no ckpt, post_train_verify skipped — produces nothing usable.

_updates.change: MLX/stdlib-fallback backend captured as pod-install heredoc in checklist §2.5. Repo-commit approach abandoned per HEXA-FIRST .gitignore **/*.py ban.

Pytorch backend (real PEFT LoRA on small base model, gpt2 default) captured as pod-install heredoc in checklist §2.5.b. End-to-end recipe + operator promotion step documented. P12 architectural surface now complete — both mlx (§2.5) and pytorch (§2.5.b) install patterns laid out. Status remains PENDING_ARCHITECTURAL_BACKEND until a live pod runs the full train → verify → promote cycle.

issues_resolved_p1.root_cause: pod_bootstrap_sequence referenced `https://<R2>/hexa-linux-x86_64` placeholder. hexa-lang build/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 existed locally but had not been uploaded to R2.
issues_resolved_p1.impact: Pod mqljodjb5pqpk4 was running but couldn't auto-bootstrap until binary was fetchable.
issues_resolved_p1.resolution: anima 55bdad56 — uploaded to r2:anima-models/bin/hexa_v2_linux_x86_64 (sha256=3ff995fc8b68e3a5b9e46a803a269e03204ff0b439a668a6dfadc58acc01d496, 5.32 MiB). Added tool/fetch_hexa_binary_url.bash helper that emits 7-day presigned R2 URL on demand (anima-models is a private bucket, so presigned URLs avoid leaking long-lived R2 creds into pod env). Updated pod_bootstrap_sequence with operator-side url-gen + pod-side curl + sha256sum -c verification.

Roadmap 66 M4 Phase C.2 (__hexa_strlit_init per-module namespacing) remains the active binary-rebuild blocker for anima.

upstream_progress_ref.roadmap_65_m3_status: Phase 1 landed (hexa-lang 9bdf2336, 2026-04-23). real_args() + script_path() canonical API available additively at source. Phase 2 (duplicate argv[0] removal + 40+ caller migration from args()[2..] → real_args()) deferred as repo-wide breaking follow-on. anima workaround removal gated on Phase 2 + binary rebuild (i.e. still waiting on 66).
upstream_progress_ref.roadmap_66_m4_status: RESOLVED upstream (968900b8 · 4238b758 · 388eece8, 2026-04-23). __hexa_sl_* + __hexa_strlit_init namespacing fix landed; binary rebuilt. M4 pad_start/pad_end + codegen safety also landed.
upstream_progress_ref.roadmap_67_m5_status: In flight — subsumes hxa-20260423-002 (Linux hexa run driver) which is now closed (done). Self-hosted Linux driver will obsolete anima's tool/hexa_linux_shim.bash.
upstream_progress_ref.m4_audit_corrections: hexa-lang session confirmed .last_index_of / .rfind / .find dispatch IS WIRED in codegen_c2.hexa as of 4a5e57a5. Anima-side _last_idx_char workaround is redundant at SOURCE level but still required because self/native/hexa_v2 binary is stale (pre-4a5e57a5) and cannot be rebuilt until roadmap 66 Phase C.2 lands. Real still-missing: .pad_start / .pad_end (only pad_left/right wired), .char_code_at (orphan sym-table entry, no dispatch, no runtime fn), .byte_at (new anima-finding — tool/roadmap_status.hexa:68 uses it; zero codegen + zero runtime).
upstream_progress_ref.anima_workaround_removal_trigger: FIRED + PARTIALLY EXECUTED Mac-side. tool/an11_a_verifier.hexa _last_idx_char helper (20 LOC) deleted, 2 call sites migrated to .last_index_of() native dispatch (anima <pending-commit>). Still pending: nth_positional dup-detect branch — requires live-pod test of real_args() dispatch before removal.
upstream_progress_ref.hxa_20260423_009_audit: Upstream bug hxa-20260423-009 ('use loses interpreter builtin dispatch — real_args/starts_with') audited 2026-04-23T05:00Z. anima impact scan: grep -rln '^use ' tool/ then intersect with real_args/starts_with call sites. Result: NO at-risk files — real_args() appears only in migration-stub comments (an11_a_verifier.hexa), no .starts_with() in any `use`-file. Only `.last_index_of()` combined with `use` (phase_progression_controller.hexa), and that dispatches correctly on current Mac stage0 — empirically verified via --stage check run which hit _dirname()/last_index_of() path and wrote cert file clean. No anima workaround needed.

m3_phase2_audit.pattern: grep -rln 'let mut i = 2' tool/ then intersect with args() users
m3_phase2_audit.migration_recipe: For each matching tool: (1) replace _argv() helper body — drop `let a = args()` + `let mut i = 2` skip, substitute `let a = real_args()` + `let mut i = 0`; (2) remove the +2 offset on any subsequent a[i] accesses inside argv building. Verify via file's own --selftest or --help path. Safe on Mac (stage0 self/native/hexa_v2 rebuilt today with canonical real_args() dispatch). On pod (rebuilt Linux binary, sha 42d01578..., in R2) — verify on next pod launch before bulk-migrate.
m3_phase2_audit.recommendation: DEFERRED to a dedicated session. Bulk migration of 80 files as end-of-session minimum-path is out-of-scope; each file needs individual selftest verification. Captured here so the next session can execute as a single-purpose batch.

convergence.stage2_infra_smoke_note: Infrastructure validated 2026-04-23T12:20Z: 4 H100 pods launched (p1 a7c30v98 / p2 y78m4i37 / p3 iahau1lc / p4 czennv3d), bootstrap OK (I7-I9), phi_extractor_ffi_wire --roundtrip-only emitted 4 phi_p<N>_live.json, cross_result.json verdict=PASS (6/6 pairs, substrate_indep=true trivially). CAVEAT: synthetic LCG fixture is path-agnostic — all 4 paths produced identical L2=6.90837. Real substrate_indep claim requires h_last FFI pod-adapter loading actual residual streams per model (Qwen/Llama/Ministral/Gemma). #10 remains planned until real-model run. Total burn ~$1.20.
convergence.stage2_real_run_note: REAL RUN 2026-04-23T12:27Z-12:39Z (4 H100 pods, ~12 min wall, $2.80 burn est). Wrote h_last FFI pod-adapter (/tmp/h_last_real.py heredoc: AutoModelForCausalLM + bf16 + output_hidden_states[-1][0,-1,:] + 16-stride projection over 16 canonical prompts). Model substitutions (original manifest models gated/multimodal): p2 meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B → mistralai/Mistral-7B-v0.1, p3 mistralai/Ministral-3-14B → Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B, p4 google/gemma-4-31B → mistralai/Mistral-Nemo-Base-2407. VERDICT=FAIL (0/6 pairs raw + 0/6 normalized). Raw L2: p1=1.95 p2=41.95 p3=3.45 p4=7.79 — architecture-specific residual scales differ ~20×, normalized cos_sim spans -0.48 to +0.32. Scientific interpretation: naive 16-stride projection is too crude; substrate_indep claim needs aligned/projective metric (Procrustes/CCA) or task-trained Φ-preserving objective. #10 stays planned.
